text:
To find the total distance that Alisa and Stanley biked, you can follow these steps:

First, calculate the distance Alisa biked:
\[ \text{Distance Alisa biked} = \text{Speed} \times \text{Time} \]
\[ \text{Distance Alisa biked} = 12 \, \text{miles per hour} \times 4.5 \, \text{hours} \]
\[ \text{Distance Alisa biked} = 54 \, \text{miles} \]

Next, calculate the distance Stanley biked:
\[ \text{Distance Stanley biked} = \text{Speed} \times \text{Time} \]
\[ \text{Distance Stanley biked} = 10 \, \text{miles per hour} \times 2.5 \, \text{hours} \]
\[ \text{Distance Stanley biked} = 25 \, \text{miles} \]

Now, add the distances together to find the total distance:
\[ \text{Total distance} = \text{Distance Alisa biked} + \text{Distance Stanley biked} \]
\[ \text{Total distance} = 54 \, \text{miles} + 25 \, \text{miles} \]
\[ \text{Total distance} = 79 \, \text{miles} \]

So, Alisa and Stanley biked a total of 79 miles.
